## Summary

This PR removes the current dual-silo public-site behavior where the production homepage and Revise page are served through static `public/marketing-export` HTML rewrites instead of the Next.js App Router.

It replaces the static-export entry points with native App Router pages, repairs the public navigation model, and creates first-class routes for the trust/resource/legal destinations that were previously dead anchors or disconnected static pages.

## Problem

The live site currently behaves like two products:

- `/` is rewritten to `/marketing-export/main/index.html`.
- `/revise` is rewritten to `/marketing-export/revise/index.html`.
- `app/page.tsx` and `app/revise/page.tsx` are redirect safety nets back into `marketing-export` rather than real pages.
- Public navigation can point users into dead anchors or disconnected static surfaces.
- Middleware still treats `/marketing-export` as a public product surface.

This is why clicking public nav items can feel inconsistent: some routes enter the real app shell, while others remain trapped in the static marketing export.

## What changed

### Route consolidation

- Removed `next.config.mjs` rewrites for:
  - `/` → `/marketing-export/main/index.html`
  - `/revise` → `/marketing-export/revise/index.html`
- Replaced `app/page.tsx` with a native routed homepage.
- Replaced `app/revise/page.tsx` with a native routed Revise landing page.
- Repointed legacy preview/mock routes:
  - `/marketing-preview/main` → `/`
  - `/marketing-preview/revise` → `/revise`
  - `/mock/pricing-premium` → `/pricing`

### Canonical header IA

Implements the simplified governed-OS header model:

- RevisionGrade logo → `/`
- Evaluate → `/evaluate`
- Revise → `/revise`
- Reliability → `/reliability`
- Resources dropdown:
  - FAQs → `/resources`
  - Methodology → `/methodology`
  - Editorial Doctrine → `/reliability`
- Pricing → `/pricing`
- Sign In → `/login` for logged-out users
- Dashboard → `/dashboard` for logged-in users
- Pipeline remains admin-only

Core product actions stay top-level. Reference material is grouped under Resources.

### Public destination routes

Adds or expands native routes for:

- `/resources` — FAQ++ author resources route
- `/reliability` — Instrument / Engine / Methodology trust route
- `/methodology` — public editorial methodology route
- `/queue` — public handoff page that directs signed-in users to dashboard queue
- `/privacy` — placeholder legal route, counsel-review required before launch
- `/terms` — placeholder legal route, counsel-review required before launch
- `/contact` — public contact/private-beta handoff route

### Shared shell

- Adds `components/SiteFooter.jsx`.
- Renders the footer from `app/layout.jsx` so public pages inherit one footer source.
- Footer links use clean App Router destinations, not static HTML or hash anchors.

### Middleware alignment

- Adds the new public trust/legal/resource routes to `publicPaths`.
- Removes `/marketing-export` from public middleware exceptions.
- Keeps protected app/workflow prefixes unchanged (`/dashboard`, `/evaluate`, `/workbench`, `/admin`, `/reports`, etc.).

### Regression guard

Updates `__tests__/lib/evaluation/architecture-invariants.test.ts` so it now asserts:

- Homepage is native, not a redirect to `marketing-export`.
- `next.config.mjs` does not rewrite public routes into `marketing-export`.
- Middleware does not treat `/marketing-export` as a public route.
- New trust/legal routes are recognized as public.

## Files changed

- `next.config.mjs`
- `app/page.tsx`
- `app/revise/page.tsx`
- `app/resources/page.tsx`
- `app/reliability/page.tsx`
- `app/methodology/page.tsx`
- `app/queue/page.tsx`
- `app/privacy/page.tsx`
- `app/terms/page.tsx`
- `app/contact/page.tsx`
- `app/layout.jsx`
- `components/HeaderNav.jsx`
- `components/SiteFooter.jsx`
- `middleware.ts`
- `app/marketing-preview/main/page.tsx`
- `app/marketing-preview/revise/page.tsx`
- `app/mock/pricing-premium/page.tsx`
- `__tests__/lib/evaluation/architecture-invariants.test.ts`

## Scope boundaries

This PR intentionally does **not** change:

- Evaluation pipeline logic
- Scoring
- Prompt behavior
- Worker behavior
- Supabase migrations
- Job state machine semantics
- Auth protection for app/workflow routes
- Runtime timeout settings

## Validation checklist

Required before merge:

- [ ] `npm run build`
- [ ] `npx jest __tests__/lib/evaluation/architecture-invariants.test.ts --runInBand`
- [ ] Manual route check on preview deployment:
  - [ ] `/`
  - [ ] `/revise`
  - [ ] `/resources`
  - [ ] `/reliability`
  - [ ] `/methodology`
  - [ ] `/pricing`
  - [ ] `/queue`
  - [ ] `/privacy`
  - [ ] `/terms`
  - [ ] `/contact`
- [ ] Browser URL check: public nav must route to clean URLs, not `#pricing`, `#resources`, or `/marketing-export/...`.
- [ ] Confirm unauthenticated `/dashboard`, `/evaluate`, and `/workbench` still route through the intended auth/private-beta flow.

## Known notes

- `main` advanced after this branch was created, so update the branch from `main` before final merge if GitHub requires it.
- `/privacy` and `/terms` are intentionally placeholder legal pages. They fix route integrity now but require counsel-approved copy before public launch.
- This PR does not delete `public/marketing-export`. Deletion should be a follow-up once preview proves no runtime dependency remains.

## No-Pipeline-Impact

true
